leads.name: Barinder Lalria
leads.headline: Freelance Cost and Value Engineering Consultant I Enabling organisations to become ‘cost conscious’.
leads.comment: Hi Jawad, great post. Totally relate to it. Was at PA Consulting for 19 years. Just came out and looking to re-train my mind.
leads.reason: 19 years at PA Consulting, just left, explicitly says looking to re-train their mind — classic forcing moment of going independent after long tenure, with clear seniority and current need.

leads.name: Beltrán Simó
leads.headline: Obsessed with growth | Former McK partner | Senior Advisor | TMT expert |
leads.comment:
Jawad Abdulsamad, I made a similar move 2 years ago, and most of what you say is 100%. The biggest truth is the 100-page decks vs. a solution in days and execution. I wrote something similar some months ago. 

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/beltrasimo_the-golden-cage-and-what-ive-learned-since-activity-7346201024774209536-tcKo?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAAVBjgBNN0-UAjghV0eaKZpX3XIypsrOZo
leads.reason: Former McKinsey partner who made a similar move 2 years ago — very senior with real transition, though the move is 2 years old making it somewhat retrospective rather than acute.

leads.name: Gary David Armstrong
leads.headline: Partner & Executive Search | Helping law firms de-risk lateral hires through data, due diligence & research | UK, Offshore & International
leads.comment: They say that the brightest light comes from a burning bridge and from someone who built a business without a plan B - I can say hand on heart it was for me, the only way to do it.
leads.buyer_type: executive search partner who left Big 4 consulting to go independent
leads.reason: First-person account of leaving Big 4 consulting to build own business with no plan B — classic went-independent forcing moment, senior headline (Partner), though the post is somewhat retrospective and motivational rather than expressing current vulnerability.

leads.name: Dariya Vyshnevska
leads.headline: Scaling B2B revenue with psychology-led sales systems. Helping teams close without pressure | Ex-Google, Ex-Grammarly
leads.comment: This feels like the in-between phase people rarely talk about. I’m in a similar reset right now. less about chasing numbers, more about building something I won’t want to run away from in a year.
leads.reason: First-person vulnerability: 'I'm in a similar reset right now... building something I won't want to run away from' — active forcing moment, ex-Google/Grammarly shows seniority and means.

leads.name: Hunter K. Jones, MA, MS
leads.headline: Founder & CEO | Business Development & Sales | Investigations & Operations | Federal Agent | US Air Force | Global Security | Insider Threat | PM | Strategist | Servant Leader | Veteran Advocate | Coach & Mentor
leads.comment: Kyle Poyar…great stuff. Look forward to networking with you in the future as I am year one into my consulting LLC. Crawl, walk, run for me as I still have a FT job. Keep doing your thing!!
leads.reason: Founder & CEO in year one of consulting LLC while still holding a full-time job — active forcing moment of career transition with real stakes.

leads.name: Chris Tottman
leads.headline: Partner at Notion Capital
leads.comment: Congratulations 👏I'm a massive fan as you know 💙 my first day today moving from "all free" to "free + paid" 😳🤞
leads.reason: Partner at Notion Capital describing their own first day transitioning from all-free to paid content — real vulnerability about a live transition with professional stakes.

leads.name: Yannik Hellmich
leads.headline: Product • Founder • ex-Meta
leads.comment:
Congrats Sven! Taking the leap is always terrifying, especially completely solo. The biggest drawback for me personally was how isolating it can be to work solo on your own company. That's something I'd never do again and look for a co-founder. 

And definitely second learning technical skills for people who have none or little. In basically any discipline it will make sure you can provide more value directly or in the very least better understand a part of your discipline that is otherwise is done by other people. 

For me this was picking up Game Development (C#), as well as SQL and the associated tools of that ecosystem. Huge bonus in any conversation where you exchange with other experts.
leads.reason: First-person vulnerability about how isolating solo founding is, saying they'd never do it again — ex-Meta founder describing real professional pain from going independent, active forcing moment.
leads.source_author:
🎉 12 Months of running my own Consulting Business: The story about a 1-person company👇

It is the first of April in 2024. I officially started my consulting business. Every document is handed in. I… | Sven Jürgens | 38 comments

leads.name: Matei C.
leads.headline: Sales capability gaps cost you quota | Hire me as VP of Sales, Operating Partner
leads.comment: Interesting. Also 1 year of XP as an independent revenue operating partner and I see lots of similar lessons and patterns 👨‍🍳 Elric Legloire
leads.reason: 1 year into going independent as a revenue operating partner (VP Sales-level positioning), reflecting on patterns and lessons — classic independent transition forcing moment with senior means.
leads.source_author: From B2B SaaS to Solo: What I Learned in 1 Year | Elric Legloire posted on the topic | LinkedIn

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts celebrating or reflecting on the one-year anniversary of going independent or starting a consulting practice
search_queries: one year ago I left my job started consulting anniversary
12 months since I went independent what I learned
first year running my own practice lessons reflection

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts where people share their experience leaving corporate to go independent
search_queries: left corporate started my own consulting LinkedIn
went independent hardest part nobody tells you
career change at 40 starting over professional

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts celebrating 2, 3, or 5 year anniversaries of running an independent consulting or coaching practice
search_queries: two years ago I started my own consulting practice best decision
three years since I went independent never going back corporate
five year anniversary my consulting business what a journey

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts from senior professionals announcing they are now offering fractional C-suite services after leaving full-time roles
search_queries: excited to announce I'm now a fractional CMO CFO COO
launching my fractional executive practice after years corporate
going fractional best move of my career helping multiple companies

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts from people looking back on leaving corporate and declaring it was the best decision, written from a place of success not uncertainty
search_queries: leaving corporate was the best decision I ever made here's why
quit my executive job two years ago would never go back
walked away from six figures to build my own thing no regrets

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts from independent consultants celebrating the milestone of hiring their first employee or growing from solo to a small team
search_queries: started as a solo consultant just hired my first employee growing
went from one person to a small team my consulting firm is growing
building a real business hired my first team member after going solo

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts from independent professionals sharing year-end or mid-year business reviews of their practice with revenue or client milestones
search_queries: year in review my consulting practice revenue clients lessons learned
halfway through my second year independent here's what the numbers look like
end of year reflection solo consulting business growth grateful

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts announcing they just quit their job to go independent or start their own practice
search_queries: just resigned starting my own consulting practice next month
handed in my notice going independent launching my own thing
last day at my corporate job tomorrow I start for myself

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts reflecting on two or three years since going independent with lessons and growth
search_queries: two years since I left corporate to go independent what changed
three years running my own practice looking back biggest lessons
second anniversary of starting my consulting business what I learned

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts celebrating matching or exceeding their previous salary as an independent
search_queries: matched my corporate salary as independent consultant first year revenue
made more on my own than my old six figure job going solo
exceeded my corporate compensation as an independent first year income

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts celebrating a breakthrough client or project win after going independent
search_queries: landed my biggest client since going independent solo consultant win
first major project on my own after leaving corporate milestone
signed my dream client six months after going independent celebration

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts about leaving corporate specifically for autonomy, family time, or lifestyle design
search_queries: left my executive job for more time with family no regrets independent
quit corporate for autonomy built a practice around my life not the other way
chose freedom over title went independent to design my own schedule

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts where senior professionals announce their last day or resignation and signal they are starting something independent
search_queries: today was my last day after years starting my own thing next chapter
just resigned from my role going independent building something new
officially left corporate starting my own consulting practice excited terrified

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts reflecting on the six-month mark of running an independent practice with honest lessons
search_queries: six months since I went independent here is what actually happened
half a year into my solo consulting practice honest update lessons
6 months ago I left to start my own thing what surprised me

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts from experienced independents sharing hard-won advice for people considering the leap
search_queries: what I wish someone told me before going independent consulting advice
thinking about leaving corporate here is what nobody tells you independent
advice for anyone about to go solo from someone who did it lessons

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts where people who were laid off chose to build their own practice instead of finding another job
search_queries: got laid off decided to start my own consulting practice instead
after my layoff I chose to go independent best decision
was let go and instead of job searching started my own thing solo

strategies.objective: LinkedIn posts where professionals left a business partnership or cofounded firm to go truly solo
search_queries: left my business partner going completely solo on my own now
dissolved our partnership starting my own independent practice fresh
walked away from the firm I cofounded building solo practice
